Name: Deepa pal

Advanced Accounting
Portionwise Test - 1

Q.2

i) Share application money received classified under equity & liabilities as per schedule III of the Companies Act, 2013.

ii) Share option outstanding account classified under equity & liabilities.

iii) Unpaid debentures matured and interest accrued thereon classified under other current liabilities.

iv) Unpaid liability on shares and other partly paid investment classified under current asset.

Q.3

The exemption available for SMC:
1) A company not listed or are not in the process of listing in any recognised stock exchange.
2) An insurance co, banking co etc.

Q.9 As per Accounting Standard 24, a discontinuing operation is a component of an enterprise being disposed through a single plan and indicate a separate major line of business and geographical area of an enterprise and distinguished component for financially & operation for reporting purpose.

A component may be disposed substantially (i.e. cut off) or piecemeal or terminating through abandonment

Following are the disclosure & presentation requirement of discontinuing operations
1) Specify the operation of the enterprise which discontinued
2) Specify the date and a period of discontinued need component
3) The carrying amount of assets and liabilities on the date of discontinued operation.
4) Specify Income & Expenses on that date.

Q.10

Quarter I (350 - 315) 35
II 150
III (200 - 50) 150
IV 200
Total Earnings for fy 2017-18. 535

Brought forward losses and current year losses should be deducted from earning

Q.8.

Com of Net Prof for Equity shareholders

Profit after tax  90,00,000
less: Preference dividend (10% X 8%/)

82,00,000

Com of weighted average no. of equity shares (WANES)

Equity shares  10,00,000
(+) Bonus Issue \[ \frac{old}{10} \times \frac{new}{5} \]  5,00,000

fresh Issue \[ \frac{2,00,000 \times 3}{12} \]  50,000
15,50,000

Basic EPS 2021-22
NP for Equity shareholders  82,00,000
÷ WANES  15,50,000

5.29

Adjusted EPS 2020-21
NP for Eq shareholders  82,00,000
÷ WANES  15,00,000

5.46

Reported EPS for 20-21
Net profit-reported EPS x WANES
for ESH = 62.30 x 10,00,000 = 6,23,00,000

EPS = \[ \frac{6,23,00,000}{15,00,000} \]

= 4.15

Q.1 As per AS18 'Related party disclosure', An individual has interest in the voting power that gives them control or significant influence over the enterprises and has financial and operating decision policy decision, then shall be considered as related party.

In the given case, SP. hotels limited enters an agreement with Mr. A for running the day to day management of the hotel, although he does not own any voting power in SP hotels limited.

Conclusion :- If Mr. A entered into agreement for running the day to day management of the hotel, which all financial and operating policy taken by BOD, although he does not have any voting power, and hence, he shall not be considered as related party.

Q.2 As per AS 17, each segment result is 10% or more of revenue, profit minus expenses (i.e. combined profit of all profit making segment or combined loss of all loss making segment whichever is higher in absolute term).

|        | A      | B      | C      | Total  |
|--------|--------|--------|--------|--------|
| Directly revenue | 500000 | 300000 | 100000 | 700000 |
| Enterprise revenue | 50000  | 40000  | 20000  | 110000 |
| Revenue from tran. with other segment | 110000 | 75000  | 150000 | 335000 |
| (–) Operating expenses | 300000 | 150000 | 75000  | 525000 |
| Segment result | 360000 | 265000 | 195000 | 820000 |

Sure, here is the extracted handwritten text:

```

Expenses on transfer  81000  58000  112000  251000
with other segment

Segment result  244000  170000  69000  492000

Conclusion - All segment A, B & C are reportable
segment as per AS 18.
```

2) False -

A specific disclosure is required if fundamental accounting assumptions are not followed in presentation and preparation of financial statements.

3) True

All significant accounting policies are form part of the financial statement.

4) False.

The amount by which any item is affected by such change is not ascertainable, wholly or in part, then the fact need to be indicated.

5) True

For in multiple enterprises various accounting policies exist, one accounting policy is not applicable to all.
